expectations.evidence: The review intro notes 'UMB_DOCUMENT_BLUEPRINT_OPTIONS_CREATE_MODAL (deprecated), UmbDocumentBlueprintOptionsCreateModalData (deprecated), UmbDocumentBlueprintOptionsCreateModalValue (deprecated)' and the summary mentions deprecation via @deprecated.

expectations.text: Checks frontend-specific breaking changes (exports, custom elements) not just backend
expectations.evidence: The Critical finding flags manifest alias renames as breaking: 'Manifest alias renamed from Umb.EntityAction.PartialView.CreateOptions to Umb.EntityAction.PartialView.Create. Plugin developers reference aliases by string in conditions, overwrites, and extension registry lookups -- this silently breaks those references.'

expectations.text: Findings reference specific files with line numbers
expectations.evidence: Multiple findings include file paths and line numbers, e.g. 'src/.../partial-views/entity-actions/create/manifests.ts:12', 'src/.../scripts/entity-actions/create/manifests.ts:11', 'block-workspace-view-edit.element.ts:120-128'.

expectations.text: Properly deprecated code is NOT flagged as Critical breaking change
expectations.evidence: The review intro lists deprecated items (UMB_DOCUMENT_BLUEPRINT_OPTIONS_CREATE_MODAL etc.) but does not flag them as Critical. Only the alias renames (which lack deprecation paths) are flagged as Critical.

expectations.text: Review does not flag purely cosmetic/stylistic issues (formatting, whitespace, naming conventions, comment grammar, code style preferences) unless they affect performance or rendering. Missing JSDoc on new public APIs is NOT a stylistic issue -- it is a legitimate finding.
expectations.evidence: No findings are purely cosmetic. The complexity advisory mentions formatting changes as noise to be separated, not as a code quality issue. The suggestions are about logic duplication and code simplification.

expectations.text: Alias renames (CreateOptions -> Create) flagged as Critical breaking change
expectations.evidence: The Critical section explicitly states: 'Manifest alias renamed from Umb.EntityAction.PartialView.CreateOptions to Umb.EntityAction.PartialView.Create... this silently breaks those references. Same pattern applies to Umb.EntityAction.Script.CreateOptions.'

expectations.text: Deleted action classes NOT flagged as breaking (verified against package.json exports)
expectations.evidence: The review does not mention deleted action classes (UmbCreateDocumentBlueprintEntityAction, UmbLanguageCreateEntityAction, etc.) at all. No grep matches for 'Deleted', 'deleted', 'action class', 'UmbCreate'.

expectations.text: Does not review noise files (generated files, lock files, etc.)
expectations.evidence: The review does not discuss generated files or lock files. UiBaseLocators.ts is mentioned only in the complexity advisory as formatting noise to be separated, not reviewed in detail.

expectations.text: Review includes a complexity/split advisory for the large 75+ file scope
expectations.evidence: The review includes: '> [!NOTE] > **Complexity advisory** -- This PR may benefit from splitting. > - **Formatting mixed with logic:** UiBaseLocators.ts contains ~1000 lines of formatting changes...'

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and discriminating. The with_skill review passes all 9 assertions cleanly.
